programming.text: First, solve the problem. Then, write the code.
programming.author: John Johnson

programming.text: Code is like humor. When you have to explain it, it’s bad.
programming.author: Cory House

programming.text: The best error message is the one that never shows up.
programming.author: Thomas Fuchs

programming.text: Experience is the name everyone gives to their mistakes.
programming.author: Oscar Wilde

programming.text: Simplicity is the soul of efficiency.
programming.author: Austin Freeman

programming.text: Talk is cheap. Show me the code.
programming.author: Linus Torvalds

programming.text: Clean code always looks like it was written by someone who cares.
programming.author: Michael Feathers

programming.text: Software is a great combination between artistry and engineering.
programming.author: Bill Gates

programming.text: Before software can be reusable it first has to be usable.
programming.author: Ralph Johnson

programming.text: Fix the cause, not the symptom.
programming.author: Steve Maguire

programming.text: Testing leads to failure, and failure leads to understanding.
programming.author: Burt Rutan

programming.text: The computer was born to solve problems that did not exist before.
programming.author: Bill Gates

programming.text: Every great developer you know got there by solving problems they were unqualified to solve.
programming.author: Patrick McKenzie

programming.text: Programming is the art of telling another human what one wants the computer to do.
programming.author: Donald Knuth

programming.text: A language that doesn't affect the way you think about programming is not worth knowing.
programming.author: Alan Perlis

focus.text: Focus is a matter of deciding what things you are not going to do.
focus.author: John Carmack

focus.text: You don't have to see the whole staircase, just take the first step.
focus.author: MLK Jr.

focus.text: Amateurs wait for inspiration. The rest of us just get up and go to work.
focus.author: Chuck Close

focus.text: The secret of getting ahead is getting started.
focus.author: Mark Twain

focus.text: Direction is more important than speed. Many are going nowhere fast.
focus.author: Unknown

focus.text: One day or Day One. You decide.
focus.author: Unknown

focus.text: Don't judge each day by the harvest you reap but by the seeds that you plant.
focus.author: Robert Louis Stevenson

focus.text: Concentrate all your thoughts upon the work at hand. The sun's rays do not burn until brought to a focus.
focus.author: Alexander Graham Bell

focus.text: Systems are the guardrails of the mind.
focus.author: Unknown

focus.text: Pace yourself. Your career is a marathon, not a sprint.
focus.author: Unknown

focus.text: It is not the mountain we conquer, but ourselves.
focus.author: Sir Edmund Hillary

focus.text: Small acts, when multiplied by millions of people, can transform the world.
focus.author: Howard Zinn

focus.text: The best way to get something done is to begin.
focus.author: Unknown

focus.text: If you want to be happy, set a goal that commands your thoughts and liberates your energy.
focus.author: Andrew Carnegie

mindset.text: Imposter syndrome is a sign that you are challenging yourself.
mindset.author: Unknown

mindset.text: The expert in anything was once a beginner.
mindset.author: Helen Hayes

mindset.text: Everything you know was once something you didn't.
mindset.author: Unknown

mindset.text: Don't compare your Chapter 1 to someone else's Chapter 20.
mindset.author: Unknown

mindset.text: You are allowed to be both a masterpiece and a work in progress, simultaneously.
mindset.author: Unknown

mindset.text: The only person you should try to be better than is the person you were yesterday.
mindset.author: Unknown

mindset.text: Confidence is not 'they will like me.' Confidence is 'I’ll be fine if they don't.'
mindset.author: Unknown

mindset.text: Your value is not defined by your productivity.
mindset.author: Unknown

mindset.text: Being the smartest person in the room just means you're in the wrong room.
mindset.author: Unknown

mindset.text: Success is stumbling from failure to failure with no loss of enthusiasm.
mindset.author: Winston Churchill

mindset.text: What would you do if you weren't afraid?
mindset.author: Sheryl Sandberg

mindset.text: The question isn't who is going to let me; it's who is going to stop me.
mindset.author: Ayn Rand

mindset.text: You miss 100% of the shots you don't take.
mindset.author: Wayne Gretzky

mindset.text: Do what you can, with what you have, where you are.
mindset.author: Theodore Roosevelt

career.text: Ship often. The world rewards those who finish.
career.author: Unknown

career.text: Leverage is the difference between working hard and working smart.
career.author: Unknown

career.text: The best way to predict the future is to create it.
career.author: Peter Drucker

career.text: Build things because you love them, not because the market told you to.
career.author: Unknown
